flop_penalty.anythingOff: Whether it fired or not it didn't feel dramatic enough.

mood_recording_variance.anythingOff: Mood doesn't swing much. I played for about 20 weeks and mood was generally stable.
designerAnswers: I think because mood is generally stable - you don't get to a place where you experience any true volatility.
I think in theory it's good and based in realism.

Same as previous - energy doesn't swing much that for the most part you can just play the game as normal. I would ask - what does energy tie to in real life? Artist energy should feel more dyamic, tied to all they do.
Yes it should be tied to touring and recording - in general when an artist does anything "active"

hype_pools_premarketing.anythingOff: The one thing that still is a bit odd in the UI is the hype section in the dashboard that has BUILDING and draining - while in their it's good - it seems very general.
designerAnswers: It is somewhat distinct - but I think when planning a release there are opportunities to describe if there will be any banked hype applied, I also think finding ways to tell the once the marketing is going how much hype/awareness is being added week to week could be good.
As listed in #6 - some numbers could be good but honestly not a requierment

The system is working really well. I think the side event INSIDE the weekly results make it feel less like an "OH MY!" | I think in general doing a working session to go through the existing meetings and come up with better scenarios is an opportunity - but in general the system is much improved.
I think the side even should be MANDATORY and it should take up a focus slot for the week. Then i think it could feel more dramatic/important.
I think it works great. I think in the section that says something like "a choice is waiting" having a preview of what it is would be better.

oneKnobChange: Reputation gain feels aggresive and quick. Creative Capital needs to also be revisited - as gaining CC is a bit of a struggle.
